task: Built task queue system for multi-developer coordination with auto-drain on pull, committed Stages 3-4 (team commands and task queue infrastructure)
discussion_summary: Session completed the task queue feature allowing developers to queue work for teammates across sessions. User asked critical follow-up questions about file sharing, task assignment inference, and auto-drain mechanics—indicating readiness to move from manual `askr task {dev-name}` commands to implicit task inference from Claude Code discussions. The system is now functional but needs enhancement to support natural language task assignment and automatic inference from context.

accomplishments.what: Task queue directory and queue file created for bippin user
askr task list and askr task queue commands tested and verified working
Fixed timestamp edge case in team display (negative time → 'active now')
Stage 3 (task queue infrastructure) committed as a8c88d9

next_actions.action: Design task inference system: define syntax/convention for implicit task assignment in Claude Code (e.g., '@lochan: do XYZ' or special comment markers) so tasks can be inferred from discussion context rather than explicit CLI calls
next_actions.why: User explicitly rejected manual `askr task {dev-name}` workflow and asked if assignment should be 'inferrable' from discussion—this is the core UX gap blocking adoption

next_actions.action: Implement auto-drain mechanism: define when/how task queues are automatically processed on git pull (e.g., post-pull hook, daemon polling, or manual drain command) and what 'draining' means (notification, auto-assignment, log entry)
next_actions.why: User asked 'how are these tasks going to get auto-drained post pull?' indicating this is a critical missing piece of the workflow

next_actions.action: Document file sharing model: clarify which files are shared (askr_state/tasks/*, .gitattributes) vs. unique per developer, and how conflict prevention works when both developers edit task queues simultaneously
next_actions.why: User asked 'what files will be shared with me and the co-founder and what will be unique to us'—this needs explicit documentation

next_actions.action: Add task metadata schema: extend task queue format to capture assignee, context (file/line), inferred intent, and status so tasks carry semantic meaning beyond plain text
next_actions.why: Current queue is append-only text; inference and auto-drain require structured task objects

next_actions.action: Test multi-developer scenario: simulate bippin and lochan both pulling, queuing tasks for each other, and verify no conflicts and proper auto-drain behavior
next_actions.why: System has been tested single-user; multi-developer edge cases (concurrent queue writes, stale pulls) need validation

decisions.decision: Task queue stored as append-only text file in askr_state/tasks/queue_{username}.md rather than structured JSON
decisions.reason: Append-only format prevents merge conflicts; union merge strategy in .gitattributes handles concurrent writes safely

decisions.decision: Manual CLI command `askr task {dev-name} 'task text'` implemented as MVP before inference layer
decisions.reason: Establishes working baseline; inference can be layered on top without breaking existing queue format

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Continuing to use explicit `askr task {dev-name} 'do this'` as the primary task assignment workflow
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: won't it be inferrable? like let's assign lochan this task I say in claude code after some discussion regarding XYZ, it's just that we have to use the proper n[aming convention]
user_rejected_decisions.domain: task assignment UX and CLI interface

failed_approaches.approach: Testing task queue with incomplete bash commands (truncated strings in tool calls)
failed_approaches.reason: Commands were cut off mid-execution; had to re-run with full syntax to verify functionality

relational_files.why: Main CLI entry point; contains task list and team commands tested this session
Initializes task queue directory structure on askr init
Defines union merge strategy for append-only task queue files to prevent conflicts
Uncommitted log file; tracks session lifecycle events

blockers: Task inference system not yet designed—unclear how Claude Code should signal task assignment to teammates
Auto-drain mechanism undefined—no specification for when/how queued tasks are processed
Task metadata schema missing—current append-only text format cannot capture assignee, context, or status needed for inference and automation
